---
title: "Erich Trieschman"
toc: false
about:
  template: trestles
  image: images/profile.jpg
  image-shape: round
  image-width: 12em
  links:
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/erichtrieschman
      target: _blank
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/etrieschman
      target: _blank
---

I'm a PhD student at MIT in the Department of Civil and Environmental
Engineering and the Laboratory for Information and Decision Systems,
advised by [Saurabh Amin](https://cee.mit.edu/people_individual/saurabh-amin/).
My research is on electricity market design, with a current focus on
financial transmission rights and the structural misalignments that
arise in market clearing.

Before MIT, I traded in ERCOT for Equilibrium Energy, modeled soil carbon at Indigo Agriculture, 
and worked in economic consulting at Analysis Group. I studied math at Williams College and statistics
and operations research at Stanford.

Outside of research I cycle, follow Arsenal more closely than is necessary, and spend time with my family.
